assertions.description: In non-IWA pages chrome.webRequest may be absent; when exposed, onHeadersReceived.addListener is the SecurityInfo hook.
A regular page without chrome.webRequest must treat SecurityInfo as unavailable rather than probing unrelated APIs.
assertions.test: typeof chrome === 'undefined' || typeof chrome.webRequest === 'undefined' ? true : typeof chrome.webRequest.onHeadersReceived?.addListener === 'function'

assertions.description: A usable SecurityInfo payload has state='secure', a certificate descriptor, and a 64-hex SHA-256 fingerprint.
assertions.test: (() => { const info = { state: 'secure', certificates: [{ fingerprint: { sha256: 'A'.repeat(64) } }] }; return info.state === 'secure' && Array.isArray(info.certificates) && /^[0-9A-F]{64}$/.test(info.certificates[0].fingerprint.sha256); })()

assertions.description: If extraInfoSpec does not request securityInfo/securityInfoRawDer, details.securityInfo is absent and the app must not pin.
assertions.test: (() => { const details = {}; const canPin = details.securityInfo?.state === 'secure' && details.securityInfo.certificates?.[0]?.fingerprint?.sha256; return !canPin; })()

assertions.description: A securityInfoRawDer-style payload can include raw certificate bytes while still exposing the fingerprint used for pinning.
assertions.test: (() => { const info = { state: 'secure', certificates: [{ rawDER: new ArrayBuffer(8), fingerprint: { sha256: 'B'.repeat(64) } }] }; return info.certificates[0].rawDER instanceof ArrayBuffer && /^[0-9A-F]{64}$/.test(info.certificates[0].fingerprint.sha256); })()
